---
title: "People"
description: "Who is behind DataCan"
---

DataCan is an independent project with no commercial sponsor, political
affiliation, or advocacy agenda (with [one deliberate
exception](open-data.qmd)). It is built and maintained in the open — the data
pipeline, the charts, and the site itself are all in the
[public repository](https://github.com/CanadaObservatory/CanadaDataObs).

<!-- TODO(owner): add who-we-are content. Suggested structure below — fill in
     names/bios/photos as you want them public; nothing here is published yet
     beyond the generic text above.

## Who we are

- **<Name>** — <one-or-two-line bio: background, why this project>

## Acknowledgements

- The statistical agencies whose work this site repackages — above all
  Statistics Canada, the OECD, and Environment and Climate Change Canada.
-->

## Get in touch

The best way to reach us is through the project's
[GitHub issues](https://github.com/CanadaObservatory/CanadaDataObs/issues) —
corrections, broken links, data questions, and suggestions for new indicators
are all welcome. If you spot a number that looks wrong, please say so: a
reference site earns trust by being correctable.
